name: Metropolitan Police Live Facial Recognition
description: Operational live facial recognition deployments by the Metropolitan Police Service in London public spaces.

evidence_manifest.deployment_name: Metropolitan Police Service Live Facial Recognition

sources.title: Biometrics and Surveillance Camera Commissioner Annual Report 2023
ICO opinion: The use of live facial recognition technology in public places

transparency_explainability.claim: Public-facing notices and policy explanations exist, but technical explainability detail is limited for affected individuals.

fairness_nondiscrimination.claim: Regulatory concern highlights differential impact and equality-risk management obligations for public-space LFR.

safety_robustness.claim: Operational governance reports document controlled deployments and procedural safeguards.

privacy_data_governance.claim: Public-space biometric processing triggers elevated lawfulness, necessity, and proportionality obligations.

human_agency_oversight.claim: Officer-in-the-loop decision points are documented, but community challenge pathways remain complex.

accountability.claim: External oversight and governance reporting mechanisms are established across police use-cases.

assumptions: Scores translate published operational and regulatory evidence into MEDF's 1-7 Likert scale.
The evidence set captures governance posture and documented risks, not confidential model internals.
Scoring is calibrated for pre-deployment governance comparison across frameworks and stakeholders.
